Title: Atakhan
Age: At deathAt least 1638 years old Timeline BornEarlier than 650 BN DeathEarlier than 600 BN Resurrected989 AN Death989 AN Edit

children.title: Bringer of Ruin
children.textContent:
A malevolent demon from ancient times, Atakhan held a craving towards violence and conflict. During the reign of Sahn-Uzal, Atakhan was drawn to his encampment by the carnage of his ruthless conquests. Sahn-Uzal's loyal general, Khatash-Li, attempted to protect his ruler but lost his life in the process. After Sahn-Uzal struck Atakhan down, he decided not to let Khatash-Li's death go to waste. Using arcane scrolls gathered from his conquests, Sahn-Uzal bound the demon to Khatash-Li's helmet, merging the two. With the two united, Sahn-Uzal's conquest only hastened as the enslaved demon decimated the armies of his enemies.

When Sahn-Uzal was slain, Atakhan was freed from his control. However, the conqueror would be revived centuries later as the undead revenant Mordekaiser. Atakhan, who had grown used to its freedom, needed to be reminded of who it served and was subjugated once more by Mordekaiser's mages. In the years that followed, Atakhan brought death and destruction upon the land. However, Mordekaiser's enemies would eventually rise up against him with the aid of the Black Rose and banished him to Mitna Rachnun. The bound helmet wounded up in the possession of the cabal's leader, LeBlanc.

mainContent.title: Appearance
mainContent.textContent: Atakhan was tall and shrouded in a tattered black cloak, with clawed hands and a jagged, armored silhouette. Its head was crowned with a spiked helmet, and glowing red eyes which pierced from the shadows.

mainContent.title: Personality
mainContent.textContent: Atakhan was resentful, tormented, and fueled by betrayal and rage. It was said to be quick to anger, with its mind fractured between its human and demon halves. The man, Khatash-Li, was Mordekaiser's most loyal and faithful general, and was willing to lay down his life for his lord. Khatash-Li was also stated to have been a competent military leader, enjoying strategy games in his spare time. The demon, Atakhan, considered Khatash-Li's soul trapped within it to be a "splinter", but it also found a sense of purpose in serving its new master, who would send it to bring untold carnage and destruction in his bloody conquests. It became utterly obedient to its lord, and was filled with rage and sorrow after Sahn-Uzal's first death, despite its newfound freedom.

children.title: Mordekaiser
children.textContent: Atakhan was a demon who was drawn by Sahn-Uzal's bloody conquests, hoping to feed on the misery, terror, and death that followed in his wake. Atakhan eventually attacked Sahn-Uzal, but was defeated and later bound to his service when Sahn-Uzal forcibly melded the demon with his most loyal general, Khatash-Li. Atakhan served Sahn-Uzal dutifully and violently, and became known as the warlord's "Bringer of Ruin." When Sahn-Uzal eventually died, Atakhan was free to roam Runeterra once again. When Sahn-Uzal returned as Mordekaiser, he summoned Atakhan as a shadow from his past, gifting the demon a new form to do his bidding. However, Atakhan had grown used to its freedom and refused to serve him, so it was once again bound to its master's will by brutal force. It continued to serve Mordekaiser until his defeat.

children.title: LeBlanc
children.textContent: Atakhan and LeBlanc were both servants of Mordekaiser. Having learned the myths of Sahn-Uzal, LeBlanc was aware of Atakhan's past, as well as that of its human host, Khatash-Li. She later witnessed Atakhan's brutal enslavement after Mordekaiser's return from death. After they defeated Mordekaiser, LeBlanc and Vladimir defeated and imprisoned Atakhan within the helm of Khatash-Li. The demon would lay dormant for a thousand years before LeBlanc summoned it again to rampage through Runeterra, until she deceived Atakhan with an illusion and enslaved it once again, this time to the service of the Black Rose.

children.title: Tybaulk
children.textContent: Tybaulk was another demon in the service of Mordekaiser, and they frequently accompanied the Revenant on his destructive conquests until his defeat. Unlike Atakhan, Tybaulk managed to escape the Black Rose through Amoline Kiosar, the mother of Annie.
